channel.title: What Is Being Built — Daily Activity Feed
channel.description: Daily updates on open-source automated research tools: commits, releases, and community activity.

item.title: Daily digest — 2026-06-21
item.description:
Our open-source research community has seen significant progress across multiple projects. Notably, the `giladfeldman/docpluck` and `giladfeldman/escicheck` repositories have both received updates, with `docpluck` enhancing version consistency and `escicheck` syncing with the latest `effectcheck` versions. Meanwhile, `Davidvandijcke/coarse` has made strides in localization and deployment fixes, and `JamieCummins/regcheck` has focused on improving the "qwen" feature and addressing issues like the "osf bug". These connections between projects demonstrate the community's collaborative spirit, and we celebrate the efforts of contributors like Gilad Feldman and David Vandijcke. As our projects continue to evolve, we look forward to seeing the impact of these updates on our research tools and the broader community.

113 commits across 6 active repositories this week.

New releases: metacheck v0.1.0-beta

Dormant repos revived: output-it-forward

Most active:

docpluck31 commits
regcheck31 commits
coarse30 commits
metacheck17 commits
escicheck3 commits

item.title: docpluck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/docpluck

The `main` branch was worked on, where the documentation and tooling were updated. Specifically, a change was made to "enforce library↔app version-pin sync" through a directive and by adding a gate to the QA, review, and deploy process. This change aims to ensure synchronization between library and application versions. The commit message suggests that this update includes both a directive and a quality assurance measure to guarantee version consistency. The update affects the documentation and tooling aspects of the repository.

2 commit(s) today:

  • 2dbdd98 [main] docs+tooling: enforce library↔app version-pin sync (directive + qa/review/deploy gate) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: escicheck: 2 commits today
item.description:
giladfeldman/escicheck

The `main` branch was the focus of the recent work, with two commits made to it. The first commit synced changes from `escicheckapp@bd6f007`, which corresponds to `effectcheck v0.6.5`, as stated in the commit message. The second commit, also on the `main` branch, synced changes from `escicheckapp@dec37b1`, which is associated with `effectcheck v0.6.4`. These commits brought the `main` branch up to date with versions `v0.6.4` and `v0.6.5` of `effectcheck`. The changes were made by Gilad Feldman, who referenced issues `#18` and `#19` in the commit messages.

2 commit(s) today:

  • a588210 [main] sync from escicheckapp@bd6f007 (effectcheck v0.6.5) (#19) — Gilad Feldman
  • 1d48c4e [main] sync from escicheckapp@dec37b1 (effectcheck v0.6.4) (#18) — Gilad Feldman

View all commits →

item.title: output-it-forward: activity update
item.description:
giovannacdelsordo/output-it-forward

The `main` branch was updated with a commit that added a Chrome Webstore link for OIF, as stated in the commit message "Add Chrome Webstore link for OIF". No other branches were worked on according to the provided commit messages. The specific change made was the addition of a link, with no other features or fixes mentioned. There were no changes to existing modules or addition of new ones, aside from the added link. The commit message explicitly mentions the addition of the Chrome Webstore link, indicating this was the sole purpose of the commit.

⚠ This repository was dormant and has recently become active again.

View all commits →
